title: Files, Descriptors, Locks, and RAII-Like C Patterns · Memory Trace: Cleanup Attribute Portability
summary: A service reload path owns heap buffers, FILE streams, descriptors, sockets, and mutexes across cancellation and errors. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem cleanup attribute portability increment in uniform resource-lifetime wrapper. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.
Pair acquisition with one idempotent or guarded release operation and a valid empty state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Cleanup Attribute Portability · Theory Cleanup Attribute Portability
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 1339. Produce bounded C evidence to release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cleanup Attribute Portability contract for the supplied data.

steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-cleanup-attribute-portability, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-cleanup-attribute-portability-c4m9jq */" for changed case 4105. It must release nested resources once in reverse acquisition order, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails.
